title: Spicy Creamy Egyptian Cream Stew
description: A velvety, spicy Egyptian-inspired stew that blends creamy richness with aromatic spices, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a medium pot, combine the lentils, water, and a pinch of salt; bring to a boil and then reduce to a simmer, cooking for about 15 minutes until the lentils are tender but not mushy.
While the lentils are cooking, heat the olive oil in a separate large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes, then stir in the minced garlic and ginger mixture.
Add the diced tomatoes, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper to the skillet, cooking for another 3 minutes until the tomatoes soften and the spices bloom.
Once the lentils are cooked, drain any excess liquid if needed and add them to the skillet, stirring to combine with the spice mixture.
Pour in the heavy cream and stir, allowing the stew to come to a gentle simmer; let it cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Season the stew with salt to taste, adjusting the heat with additional cayenne if desired.
Remove from heat and let it rest for 5 minutes so the flavors meld.
Serve hot over a bed of couscous or rice if desired, garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh parsley (optional).
